title: Queen Elizabeth II Canadian Research Fund Act
title_en: Queen Elizabeth II Canadian Research Fund Act
description: Queen Elizabeth II Canadian Research Fund Act (Queen Elizabeth II Canadian Research ...). Current to 2026-02-04.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: 1 This Act may be cited as the Queen Elizabeth II Canadian Research Fund Act .

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: 1.1 In this Act, Council means the Medical Research Council established by section 3 of the Medical Research Council Act .

provisions.title: Fund established
provisions.content: 2 There is hereby established a fund, to be known as The Queen Elizabeth II Canadian Fund to Aid in Research on the Diseases of Children (hereinafter called the Fund), which shall consist of all money, securities and other property received by the Council for the purposes of the Fund and shall include all income derived from the Fund and all gifts, bequests, appropriations and other contributions made to the Fund.

provisions.title: Purposes of Fund
provisions.content: 3 The purposes of the Fund are to assist individuals or organizations to undertake or carry on research into the diseases of children, and the causes, prevention and treatment of such diseases.

provisions.title: Appropriation to the Fund
provisions.content: 4 There is hereby appropriated for the Fund, out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, the sum of one million dollars.

provisions.chapter: Powers and Duties of the Council
provisions.title: Management and administration
provisions.content: 6 The Council shall manage and administer the Fund in accordance with and subject to this Act.

provisions.chapter: Powers and Duties of the Council
provisions.title: Payments
provisions.content: 7 The Council may make such payments out of the Fund for the purposes of the Fund as it considers desirable.

provisions.chapter: Powers and Duties of the Council
provisions.title: Investments
provisions.content: 8 The Council may invest any moneys of the Fund in bonds or other securities of the Government of Canada and may sell any such bonds and securities.

provisions.chapter: Powers and Duties of the Council
provisions.title: Advisory committees
provisions.content: 9 The Council may appoint from among its members or otherwise such advisory or other committees as it considers necessary or advisable for the administration of this Act.

provisions.chapter: Powers and Duties of the Council
provisions.title: Property
provisions.content: 10 The Council may, for the purposes of this Act, acquire by purchase, lease, gift, bequest or otherwise any real or personal property and may own, hold, sell, manage or otherwise deal therewith in such manner as it may determine.

provisions.chapter: Powers and Duties of the Council
provisions.title: By-laws
provisions.content: 11 The Council may make by-laws for regulating its proceedings and generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act.

provisions.title: Gifts to Her Majesty
provisions.content: 12 A gift or bequest to the Fund or to the Council for the purposes of the Fund shall be deemed to be a gift to Her Majesty. 13 to 15 [Repealed, 1995, c. 29, s. 72] Page Details Date modified: 2026-02-18 Justice Laws Website Constitutional Documents Related Resources Consolidated Acts and Regulations Search Help Contact Us Department of Justice Canada Family Law Criminal Justice Funding Canada's System of Justice Laws Government of Canada All contacts Departments and agencies About government Themes and topics Jobs Immigration and citizenship Travel and tourism Business Benefits Health Taxes Environment and natural resources National security and defence Culture, history and sport Policing, justice and emergencies Transport and infrastructure Canada and the world Money and finance Science and innovation Indigenous peoples Veterans and military Youth Social media Mobile applications About Canada.ca Terms and conditions Privacy Top of Page _satellite.pageBottom();
